title: SpecTestabilityEnricherOutput
description: Full output of the spec_testability_enricher agent (instance 0 only). For clear requirements: writes acceptance_criteria + evidence_type in enrichments[]. For vague requirements: emits a requirement-targeted manual_resolution_item. An empty manual_resolution_items array means all requirements are clear and all AC is in enrichments[].

enrichments.description: Per-spec AC + evidence_type for requirements that are clear enough to derive testable criteria. Must NOT include a spec_id that also appears in manual_resolution_items.

manual_resolution_items.description: Requirement-targeted items for specs whose requirement is too vague to derive testable AC. field is always 'requirement'. Must NOT include a spec_id that also appears in enrichments.

spec_id.description: spec_id of the row being enriched.

acceptance_criteria.description: Concrete, testable acceptance criteria derived from the requirement. Set to 'NA' when evidence_type is 'NA'.

evidence_type.description: Most appropriate evidence type for verifying this requirement, or NA when no evidence type applies.

criteria.description: Structured per-criterion breakdown of acceptance_criteria. Optional (still allowed empty for evidence_type='NA' specs). When non-empty and test_plan.planned_test_cases is non-empty, every planned_test_cases[].criteria_ids[] entry must reference a criterion_id from this array (prompt-enforced).

test_plan.description: Per-spec planned test cases. P6: promoted to required. planned_test_cases may be empty when evidence_type is 'NA' or criteria is empty.

criterion_id.description: Stable identifier within this spec's criteria, e.g. 'AC1', 'AC2'. Referenced by test_plan.planned_test_cases[].criteria_ids[] and downstream by amendment_packet entries.

statement.description: Given/When/Then or equivalent statement of this single criterion.

observable_signal.description: What an automated check or human inspector would observe to confirm the criterion is satisfied (specific value, status, log line, screen element, etc.).

evidence_kind.description: Category of evidence required to verify this criterion. Drives downstream review-axis demotion rules.

planned_test_cases.description: Test cases the implementer should author. Coverage rule (prompt-enforced): every criterion_id from criteria[] whose evidence_kind is unit_test or integration_test must appear in at least one planned_test_case.criteria_ids[].

test_id.description: Stable identifier within this spec's test_plan, e.g. 'T1', 'T2'.

target_file.description: Codebase-relative path to the test file the implementer should author or extend.

target_case.description: Specific test function/method/case identifier inside target_file.

criteria_ids.description: criterion_ids from this enrichment's criteria[] that this test case is intended to cover.

rationale.description: Why this test case satisfies the listed criteria. May explicitly note when a criterion's evidence_kind is manual_review or runtime_log and the test serves only as a structural anchor.

item_id.description: Unique identifier for this resolution item (e.g. TEST-001).

title.description: Short human-readable summary of why the requirement cannot produce testable AC.

spec_id.description: Identifier of the spec row with the vague requirement.

field.description: Always 'requirement'. The suggestion targets the requirement text, not AC.

untestable_reason.description: Explanation of why the requirement is too vague to derive testable AC.

suggested_improvement.description: Complete replacement text for the requirement field that would make it clear enough to derive testable AC.

suggested_test_type.description: Test type the suggested_improvement would enable.

options.description: Resolution options presented to the user.

option_id.description: accept_suggestion: apply suggested_improvement to requirement; let_agent_edit: call spec_editor for a custom rewrite; skip: leave requirement unchanged.

effect.description: Short description of what choosing this option does.
